description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the ubuntu:16.04 Docker image. Define three stages: build, test, and deploy (default order). In the build stage add a job named build that runs a before_script to apt update && apt -y install make autoconf gcc g++, then compiles helloworld.cpp with g++ -Wall --coverage -fprofile-arcs -ftest-coverage -o helloworld, lists files with ls, and stores the resulting helloworld binary as an artifact. In the test stage add a job named test that runs bash verify.sh. In the deploy stage add a job named coverage that runs a before_script to apt-get -qq update && apt-get -qq install -y make autoconf gcc g++ gcovr ggcov lcov, then lists files, recompiles helloworld.cpp with g++ -Wall -fprofile-arcs -ftest-coverage -fPIC -o helloworld, executes ./helloworld, and runs gcovr -r . to generate a coverage report; no caches, services, or conditional rules are used.
